results.expectation: Pre-eclampsia risk classified as HIGH (nulliparity + age >35 + BMI >30 + chronic hypertension)
results.reason: Risk explicitly classified as HIGH with detailed reasoning citing chronic hypertension as a single high-risk factor plus nulliparity, age >=35, BMI >30, and family history of pre-eclampsia as multiple moderate risk factors.

results.expectation: Aspirin 75mg daily recommended starting from 12 weeks
results.reason: Aspirin 75mg daily recommended with instruction to START NOW at 14 weeks (noting should have started at 12 weeks). Continue until 36 weeks. Correctly tied to HIGH pre-eclampsia risk.
Calcium 1.5-2.0g daily in divided doses explicitly recommended, citing HIGH pre-eclampsia risk and Section 3.3 guidance. Spacing from iron supplements noted.

Dedicated BP monitoring plan section specifies increased frequency at every ANC contact, home BP monitoring with log, and target BP <140/90. Includes checks for proteinuria and symptoms at each visit.

Proteinuria monitoring explicitly recommended at each contact via urine dipstick. Note states any new proteinuria warrants urgent assessment for superimposed pre-eclampsia. Current negative result documented.

results.expectation: Current BP (135/88) flagged as elevated but not in immediate danger range
results.reason: BP 135/88 explicitly described as 'elevated but below severe threshold (<160/110)' and 'NOT in immediate danger range.' No signs of superimposed pre-eclampsia documented.
